_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn to evaluate a proposed solution using criteria someone gives us.

_source.w: We learn to evaluate a proposed solution using criteria someone gives us.
y: Evaluating with criteria teaches us to judge things fairly and systematically.
This skill helps us go beyond 'I like it' to 'Here is why it works or doesn't work'.
Using criteria is how professionals assess whether solutions are good enough.
eg: We use given criteria — like safety, effectiveness and cost — to evaluate a design solution.
We check a proposed solution against a list of requirements and tick off which ones are met.
We give feedback on a classmate's work using the criteria our teacher provided.
We rank several solutions from best to worst using the same criteria for each.
We discuss why a solution might score well on one criterion but poorly on another.
We suggest improvements based on which criteria the solution did not fully meet.
